answer:
Little Bourke Street was important because the author, Fergus Hume, spent many nights there gathering material for his novel, as it was a notorious area frequented by prostitutes and criminals. This firsthand observation allowed him to authentically depict the slums and the class divide central to the story. The street's notoriety provided crucial setting details for the mystery. 

\boxed{\text{Little Bourke Street was important because the author gathered material there for his novel, as it was notorious for prostitutes and criminals, allowing him to authentically depict the slums and class divide.}}
